top_findings: CRITICAL: Zero live, indexable business listings. sitemap-listings-1.xml has 0 URLs (down from 1 at the last audit). The routing/sitemap/schema code is now genuinely correct, but every seed business is permanently tagged dataSource:"demo" and no live UI or import job can ever write a non-demo row -- the owner claim-listing form only writes to localStorage, admin approvals is disconnected mock UI, and the OSM/CSV importers have zero production wiring. Five independent specialist passes (Sitemap, Schema, Local, SXO, GEO) converged on this root cause.

HIGH: Homepage/city marketing copy ("50,000+ local businesses", "12,840 listings" for Kathmandu) is now directly contradicted by the platform's own moderation-gate banner, visible to any real user who clicks through to the one example listing -- a new, user-visible trust problem.
HIGH: A residual pre-fix duplicate-content cluster of 4 blog posts (published 2026-07-05/06) remains live at 62-66% pairwise similarity, failing the site's own current 0.55 dedup threshold.
MEDIUM: CSP ships as Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only, not enforcing -- XSS/injection mitigation from CSP is not active yet (X-Frame-Options clickjacking protection is active independently).
HIGH: LCP images on all three main templates (homepage hero, business photo, blog featured image) are missing fetchpriority="high", contributing to LCP of 2.69s-3.37s.
quick_wins: Add `priority` to the next/image LCP element on homepage hero, business-hero photo, and blog featured-image templates (one line per template)
Consolidate/redirect the 4 residual duplicate blog posts using the existing lib/blog-dedup.ts pattern
Reinstate explicit per-bot Allow rules (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, OAI-SearchBot) in robots.txt
Add QAPage JSON-LD to /questions/trekking-annapurna (currently zero schema)
Delete the dead app/[indexNowKey].txt route folder
Re-compress the homepage's Unsplash hero image (89% file-size savings available)
Create and link official social profiles from the footer (unblocks citation-directory submissions)

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Hardcoded admin credentials removed; app/login/page.tsx now uses real Supabase auth
Non-www to www 308 redirect fixed and verified live
sitemap.xml is now a clean sitemapindex with zero admin/private URL leakage across all 141 URLs
All 5 previously-missing security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, HSTS w/ preload) live site-wide
24 pages given unique title/meta via new layout.tsx metadata files, fixing the prior 33% duplicate-title issue
IndexNow now has an active submission mechanism wired into the daily blog cron

findings.title: CSP is Report-Only, not enforcing
findings.description: Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only is set but plain Content-Security-Policy is not, and no report-to/report-uri endpoint is configured to collect violation data. XSS/frame-ancestors protection from CSP is not yet active (X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN does provide independent, active clickjacking protection).
findings.recommendation: Add a reporting endpoint, monitor for a short period, then flip to enforcing Content-Security-Policy. Consider nonce/hash-based script-src and style-src before enforcing, since 'unsafe-inline' is currently present in both.

findings.title: /claim-listing H1 presence not re-verified
findings.description: Carried over from the 2026-07-11 baseline as unconfirmed rather than fixed.
findings.recommendation: Direct check needed; add an H1 if still missing.

findings.title: Dead app/[indexNowKey].txt route folder
findings.description: Folder still exists with no route.ts inside, alongside the working hardcoded-key route.
findings.recommendation: Delete for clarity.

findings.title: robots.txt dropped explicit per-bot Allow blocks
findings.description: Simplified to a single User-Agent: * rule. Functionally equivalent (wildcard covers all bots including AI crawlers) but less explicit for audit/documentation purposes.
findings.recommendation: Reinstate explicit per-bot Allow rules if the auditable signal is valued.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Embedding/topic/shingle dedup pipeline (added same-day as prior audit) holds at 8x blog volume scale (12 to 97 posts) -- 0.00-0.03 pairwise similarity across the post-fix corpus
New blog category hub thin-archive guard keeps sub-3-post archives out of the index
New-corpus posts run 800-1,400+ words of real prose across 6-9 genuine H2 sections, versus 160-410 on the original baseline posts
/authors/* desk pages now function as real topic hubs with 15-19 guides each
AI-content-transparency disclosure metadata carries through to new posts

findings.title: Residual pre-fix duplicate-content cluster (4 posts)
findings.description: 4 posts published 2026-07-05/06, right before the dedup fix landed, score 0.62-0.66 pairwise similarity against each other by the site's own current threshold (0.55) -- verbatim instances of a deterministic fallback scaffold.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate 3 of the 4 through the current pipeline, or merge into one hub post and 301 the other three via the existing duplicateBlogRedirects pattern.

findings.title: Original baseline posts remain thin
findings.description: 6 re-checked original posts show only 160-410 words of real prose once templated boilerplate is excluded -- not retroactively rewritten through the now-stronger pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Backfill the original ~14 posts through the current generation pipeline.

findings.title: Anonymous 'desk' bylines on YMYL health content
findings.description: Every post, old and new, is attributed to an Organization-typed desk byline rather than a named person with credentials. Gap now covers more health-adjacent posts than at the prior baseline.
findings.recommendation: Add a named human reviewer with real credentials for healthcare-vertical content specifically.

findings.title: Homepage and category pages remain thin; moderation gate confirmed as root cause
findings.description: Homepage is 63 words, /category/restaurants is 119 words. getEvergreenPages() (would generate data-backed 'Best X in Y' pages) is gated behind an includePreview flag and returns [] in production.
findings.recommendation: Treat as one workstream with the Critical listings-pipeline fix -- content polish cannot move this further until that unblocks.

findings.title: /authors/team duplicated paragraph
findings.description: The page's only content paragraph renders twice back-to-back.
findings.recommendation: Render once; expand with the guide-listing structure used on other desk pages.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
categories.note: No dedicated on-page specialist ran this cycle; score synthesized from Technical/Content/Schema evidence.
what_works: Title/meta hygiene now strong: 24 previously-duplicate pages have unique, descriptive titles
Canonical tags self-referencing and www-correct across sampled pages

findings.title: Core pages still thin on real on-page content
findings.description: Homepage (63 words) and category pages (119 words) are far under any reasonable page-type floor, driven by the moderation gate suppressing listing-backed content.
findings.recommendation: Same fix as the Critical listings-pipeline finding -- see Content Quality and Sitemap categories.

findings.title: /claim-listing has no H1 and only 133 words
findings.description: Undersells the product at the exact moment a prospective business owner is evaluating it.
findings.recommendation: Add an H1 and substantive copy.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: Site-wide Organization + WebSite graph intact and consistent across all sampled pages
priceRange bug fixed (correct currency-symbol format, was malformed 'Rs Rs Rs' string)
geo/GeoCoordinates now emitted whenever coordinates exist
openingHoursSpecification now maps full 7-day data via a structured weeklyHours model
BlogPosting template on blog posts remains well-built (headline, dates, author, publisher, wordCount, FAQPage)

findings.title: Zero live business listings carry LocalBusiness/Restaurant schema
findings.description: /business/newa-lahana, the baseline's flagship schema example, now emits only WebPage + BreadcrumbList. Every business listing sitewide is currently non-indexable, so the improved schema template (priceRange, geo, hours all fixed at the code level) has no live instance anywhere to validate.
findings.recommendation: Get at least one real listing through isIndexableListing() so the fixed template ships to production and can be validated in Google's Rich Results Test.

findings.title: Menu graph (hasMenu/MenuSection/servesCuisine) has no equivalent in the new schema builder
findings.description: A capability regression independent of the indexability issue -- the old static template's menu structured data has no replacement in lib/seo-auto/schema.ts.
findings.recommendation: Restore Menu/hasMenu and servesCuisine properties if menu data still exists in the Listing model.

findings.title: /questions/trekking-annapurna has zero page-specific schema
findings.description: A genuine single-question Q&A page with no WebPage, BreadcrumbList, or QAPage markup.
findings.recommendation: Add QAPage (mainEntity: Question -> acceptedAnswer: Answer) plus WebPage/BreadcrumbList.

findings.title: City hub pages still lack ItemList schema
findings.description: Carried over from the baseline, unchanged.
findings.recommendation: Extend with ItemList once real listing data exists to populate it.

findings.title: FAQPage schema retains AI/GEO value only, no Google SERP benefit
findings.description: As of 2026-05-07, Google retired FAQ rich results for all sites. Existing FAQPage blocks still carry citation/entity-resolution value for AI engines.
findings.recommendation: Keep existing FAQPage blocks; do not add new ones expecting a Google SERP feature.

categories.name: Performance
categories.note: First real Lighthouse lab measurement this cycle; prior 'score' was a manual estimate, not directly comparable.
what_works: Real TTFB measured at 70-160ms across all four pages tested, well under the 800ms good threshold
CLS well-controlled on 3 of 4 pages (business and blog both measure 0.000)
Images served via next/image with responsive srcset and automatic WebP negotiation
Brotli compression and Vercel edge delivery confirmed

findings.title: LCP images missing fetchpriority="high" across all three templates
findings.description: Homepage (2.69s LCP), business page (2.29s), and blog post (3.37s LCP) all lack fetchpriority="high" on their LCP image, confirmed via Lighthouse lcp-discovery-insight (score 0) and preload_check.py.
findings.recommendation: Add the `priority` prop to next/image on the hero/LCP image for each template. Expected 100-300ms LCP improvement.

findings.title: Homepage hero image ~89% larger than necessary
findings.description: Lighthouse flags 70,966 of 79,612 bytes as avoidable via better compression, specific to this one Unsplash source image.
findings.recommendation: Re-encode or re-source at a lower quality parameter; verify file size drops without visible quality loss.

findings.title: Total Blocking Time elevated on every page tested
findings.description: 245-455ms TBT (INP lab-proxy) on all four pages, above the 200ms good threshold. Mainthread-work-breakdown scores 0 on homepage, category, and business, dominated by layout/style work.
findings.recommendation: Cross-check against real CrUX INP field data once API access exists; profile main-thread work on card-grid pages first.

findings.title: Web-font load causes layout shift on /category/* pages
findings.description: The only CLS 'needs improvement' result (0.110) across the four pages tested, traced to a FOUT-style reflow on the 'Quick answer' block.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the homepage's font preload link also fires on category templates, or use font-display: swap with pre-sized fallback metrics.

findings.title: Blog post LCP (3.37s) is the weakest of the four templates
findings.description: Unusually large elementRenderDelay (713ms) for the featured image, compounding the shared fetchpriority gap.
findings.recommendation: Apply the priority-prop fix first; investigate render-blocking work if delay remains high after.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: Technical accessibility holds across the board -- all sampled pages fully server-rendered, no JS execution needed
Deliberate content-integrity gating (refusing to publish unverified business data) is a legitimate, if costly, trust signal
New/scaled blog posts show materially stronger GEO structure: Quick-answer blocks, FAQPage-backed FAQ sections, Source-and-fact-notes sections
The 12-URL/5-cluster duplicate-blog problem is resolved at the URL level via clean 308 redirects to canonicals
llms.txt is actively regenerated from live state (dropped a dead link, added 6 new category entries), not hand-frozen

findings.title: Zero AI-citable individual-business or named-comparison pages exist sitewide
findings.description: The one business page is noindex,nofollow; comparison pages are noindex with no named providers; a previously-live best-of page now 404s. llms.txt's business-comparison and data-backed-answers sections are present but empty.
findings.recommendation: Push a small batch (20-50) of reviewed real business records through the review pipeline so noindex can be lifted and sitemap-listings-1.xml populated -- the single biggest lever for local-intent AI citations.

findings.title: llms.txt curation covers only ~22% of live posts and has two empty section headers
findings.description: The 'Local guides' section lists only 20 of 89 live posts, missing the large mid-July wave.
findings.recommendation: Widen curation to track sitemap growth, or add one-line notes explaining the empty comparison/data-answer sections so they don't read as a bug.

findings.title: robots.txt lost explicit per-AI-bot directives
findings.description: Simplified from dedicated GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot/OAI-SearchBot blocks to a single wildcard. Net access unaffected but loses the auditable intent signal and ability to differentiate search-citation bots from training-only scrapers.
findings.recommendation: Reinstate explicit per-bot Allow rules if differentiating CCBot/anthropic-ai intent matters.

findings.title: No distinct machine-readable 'facts last verified' signal
findings.description: No structured field separate from dateModified that an AI engine could extract to gauge freshness of a specific fact.
findings.recommendation: Add once business listings are live in volume, since stale hours/prices are the highest-harm case.

categories.name: Images
categories.note: No dedicated image-optimization specialist ran this cycle; score synthesized from Performance/Visual evidence.
what_works: next/image used consistently with responsive srcset and automatic WebP negotiation
images.remotePatterns restricted to images.unsplash.com only
No broken images or overlapping elements detected across screenshots

findings.title: Homepage hero image ~89% larger than necessary
findings.description: See Performance category -- same finding, image-specific root cause.
findings.recommendation: Re-encode or re-source at lower quality parameter.

findings.title: Alt-text coverage not independently audited this cycle
findings.description: No dedicated image-alt-text crawl ran this pass.
findings.recommendation: Run a dedicated seo-images pass next cycle.

categories.name: Sitemap Architecture
what_works: sitemap.xml is now a proper sitemapindex referencing 4 clean child sitemaps
Zero admin/dashboard/account/login/register/gallery/profile URLs across all 141 live URLs
robots.txt matches app/robots.ts exactly
priority/changefreq fields removed (Google ignores both)
Blog dedup + 301 redirects + thin-category filtering all correctly wired

findings.title: Listings sitemap has 0 URLs; root cause is a missing supply pipeline
findings.description: getListingSitemapEntries() now correctly calls the real listing repository, but every seed row is permanently dataSource:"demo" and nothing live can write a non-demo row. This is the same core finding surfaced independently across Schema, Local, SXO, and GEO.
findings.recommendation: Wire claim-listing to real DB writes with a connected approval flow, and/or bulk-import real data via the existing OSM importer.

findings.title: No sitemap chunking/pagination logic
findings.description: app/sitemap-listings-1.xml/route.ts is a fixed filename with no dynamic [page] segment; will silently under-report once real listings exceed 50,000 URLs.
findings.recommendation: Build chunking concurrently with the listings-pipeline fix, not after.

categories.name: Search Experience Optimization (SXO)
what_works: Comparison/guide content topically matches real search intent for several verticals (dental, wedding planning)
Real Nepal directory/marketplace competitors validate the page type is viable when populated with real inventory

findings.title: Zero indexable individual listing pages (regressed from 1 to 0)
findings.description: Every persona score is capped by this. The 'searcher trying to find and evaluate one specific real business' persona scores 4/100.
findings.recommendation: Same fix as the Sitemap Critical finding -- this is the single highest-leverage fix for every SXO persona score.

findings.title: The one working content funnel is three disconnected silos
findings.description: Zero bidirectional links exist between any blog post and its matching city or compare-business hub.
findings.recommendation: Wire the 12-14 identified blog-to-hub link pairs -- a render-layer fix against existing data, no new content needed.

findings.title: Marketing claims contradicted by user-visible moderation-gate copy
findings.description: Homepage/category count claims ('50,000+ businesses', '12,840 listings') sit directly beside a demo listing that says, in the site's own words, it isn't indexed yet.
findings.recommendation: Scale back or clearly label counts as aspirational until substantiated by real approved listings.

findings.title: site:nepalidirectory.com returns zero results
findings.description: Independent confirmation via live search that the domain has negligible-to-no search visibility for its core content type, while five competing Nepal directories rank.
findings.recommendation: Downstream of the listings-pipeline fix; no independent action.

categories.name: Local SEO
what_works: Dynamic /business/[slug] route now exists and all 11 seed slugs return 200
getListingSitemapEntries() sources from the real listing repository, not a hardcoded literal
localBusinessSubtype() correctly maps 8 verticals, correctly avoiding the deprecated Attorney type
6 new /category/* hub pages exist alongside 8 /city/* pages -- dedicated category pages are the #1 local-organic ranking factor per Whitespark 2026
Platform's own no-NAP policy is confirmed appropriate for an online-only aggregator business model

findings.title: Zero indexable business pages in production
findings.description: Root cause changed from a code bug to a data-supply gap, but the business-facing outcome (zero local search visibility for every listed business) is unchanged.
findings.recommendation: Connect a real data source -- populated Postgres via owner/OSM/import, or run the existing enrichment pipeline against real Nepal business data.

findings.title: 4 of 6 published category verticals will get the wrong schema subtype
findings.description: hospitals, schools, it-companies, and shops match none of localBusinessSubtype()'s 8 mapped needles and will fall back to generic LocalBusiness.
findings.recommendation: Extend subtypeByCategory to cover all 6 published verticals before real listings populate them.

findings.title: No ItemList schema on city or category hub pages
findings.description: Confirmed live on /city/kathmandu and all 6 /category/* pages -- now applies to 14 pages, widened from the prior 8.
findings.recommendation: Extend both page types with ItemList once real listing data exists.

findings.title: City pages likely still ~85% templated
findings.description: Not independently re-diffed this session; carried forward from the baseline as still-open.
findings.recommendation: Re-run the text-diff check; differentiate city pages with real local specificity before scaling to more cities.

findings.title: Geo precision in seed data below 5-decimal recommendation
findings.description: Seed coordinates are mostly 4 decimal places (~11m accuracy) versus Google's recommended 5-decimal minimum (~1.1m).
findings.recommendation: Enforce 5-decimal minimum at the data-entry/import-validation layer.

categories.name: Backlinks
categories.note: Informational floor -- Tier 0 data only (Common Crawl + verification crawler, no Moz/Bing/DataForSEO keys). Expected baseline for a young, pre-launch site, not a defect.
what_works: Homepage no longer links out to example.com placeholder businesses (fixed via the same isDemoListing() gate suppressing the listings sitemap)
/attribution page's OpenStreetMap credit links correctly carry rel="nofollow"

findings.title: No measurable referring-domain footprint
findings.description: Common Crawl shows zero referring domains, re-confirmed with a forced cache-bypass fetch. Normal pre-growth state, not a penalty.
findings.recommendation: Not urgent; revisit once outreach begins.

findings.title: Zero off-site social footprint
findings.description: No Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, or YouTube links anywhere in the footer or header.
findings.recommendation: Create and link official social profiles -- zero cost, prerequisite for most citation-directory submissions.

findings.title: Tier 0 data-source ceiling
findings.description: No DA/PA, Spam Score, anchor-text distribution, or link velocity data available.
findings.recommendation: Get a free Moz API key (2,500 rows/month) -- highest-leverage improvement available at zero cost.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Connect a real listing-data supply pipeline (owner-submission write path and/or OSM bulk import) -- unblocks Sitemap, Schema, Local, SXO, and GEO simultaneously
Build sitemap chunking/pagination before real listing volume lands
Extend localBusinessSubtype() to cover hospitals, schools, it-companies, and shops before real listings populate those verticals

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Resolve the marketing-claims-vs-moderation-gate contradiction on homepage/city pages
Consolidate the 4 residual duplicate blog posts (07-05/06 cohort)
Wire blog to city-hub to compare-business internal links
Add priority prop to LCP images on homepage/business/blog templates
Re-compress the homepage's Unsplash hero image
Fix /claim-listing H1 and thin copy
Add QAPage schema to /questions/trekking-annapurna
Backfill the original ~14 baseline blog posts through the current pipeline

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Promote CSP from Report-Only to enforcing (add reporting endpoint first)
Fix font-load CLS regression on /category/* pages
Reinstate explicit per-bot Allow rules in robots.txt
Clean up llms.txt curation (empty headers, coverage gap)
Add named human reviewer byline for health-vertical content
Add ItemList schema to city/category hub pages once real listing data exists
Create and link official social profiles from the footer

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Configure Google API credentials (PageSpeed/CrUX/GSC/GA4) for real field data
Get a free Moz API key for richer backlink data
Re-run a full audit once real listings go live to validate the schema/sitemap/local fixes in production
Run a dedicated on-page and image-optimization specialist pass (not covered independently this cycle)
Re-diff city-page content uniqueness once more cities are added
